architecture.summary: The system applies Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) to attention query activations in LLMs to decompose them into interpretable sparse features. During inference, gradient-based optimization adjusts the sparse representation to align with learned class prototypes of target behaviors, steering generation without modifying the base model weights. Information flows as: forward pass → query activation extraction → SAE encoding to sparse features → gradient optimization against prototype targets → sparse feature manipulation → generation.

biblio.title: Steered Generation via Gradient-Based Optimization on Sparse Query Features

claims.statement: Manipulating attention query activations offers sharper steerability than general state interventions
Sparse query representations provide the necessary disentanglement for unified, interpretable control over both logical planning and stylistic nuance
Optimizing sparse query features enables effective navigation of rigid planning requirements (safe vs. short paths) in Textualized Gridworld
The framework steers the cognitive complexity of feedback (Bloom's Taxonomy) in a high-dimensional educational domain
Interventions on dense states can entangle distinct semantic features
